parts.answer:  The slogan must also reflect the culture aspect of life in the destination or the nature of its people – There is no slogan used here.  Language used in portraying the destination brand must be carefully considered so that visitors are not misled or confused by the brand message compared to the destination itself. Information in the Insert is clear and simple. Accept all valid responses. İstanbul. AO1 Knowledge and understanding  Brand name  Slogan/tagline  Logo  USP AO2 Application  Brand name – Istanbul is mentioned in the brand name and logo.  Slogan/tagline – Istanbul is the new cool.  Logo – the name and slogan are incorporated into the logo to ensure there is no confusion.  USP – eye-catching font and unique claim of the slogan will attract customers’ attention for being different from other brand identities seen before. Accept all valid responses. May/June 2024 Question Answer Marks tourism market for a destination. Use Table A to mark candidate responses to this question. AO1 – out of 3 marks AO3 – out of 3 marks AO4 – out of 3 marks AO1 Knowledge and understanding  Time-consuming process  You can't verify the results of qualitative research  Labour-intensive approach  Difficult to investigate causality  Qualitative research is not statistically representative  Biased  Small scale  Anecdotal, and/or lacking rigor AO3 Analysis and research  It's a time-consuming process – often involves interviews.  You can't verify the results of qualitative research – because it is opinion based.  It's difficult to investigate causality – without asking for justification and context to each response.  Qualitative research is not statistically representative– difficult to present the results succinctly.  It’s biased – opinion based.  It’s small scale – more difficult to administer.  It’s anecdotal, and/or lacking rigor – because it is difficult to ask for justification of opinion-based responses.  SMART objectives will additionally allow stakeholders to achieve exactly what they set out to achieve as they progress towards the brand launch. The objectives they agree upon will be realistic, which will help keep all of the activities and plans within budget and because they will be time- scaled, and carefully scheduled within the marketing plan for the launch, Guardians of the brand will be able to check progress at regular intervals between the original objectives and the progress made.
